items.title: Women are less likely than men to receive CPR. This surf club is looking to change that
items.summary: NSW Ambulance data shows women are 10 per cent less likely than men to receive CPR from a bystander in public, and 50 per cent less likely to get defibrillation .

items.title: Trump looks increasingly trapped over Iran as markets gyrate and oil shortage hits heartland
items.summary: Donald Trump appears to be fast running out of exits on his Iran war folly.

items.summary: A Sydney-based employment lawyer says the network who owns radio station KIIS FM acted "prematurely" when it cancelled the controversial Kyle and Jackie O Show.

items.title: Children to be banned from riding e-bikes and e-scooter devices in Queensland under proposed laws
items.summary: A raft of new rules around the use of e-mobility devices will be ushered in after the Queensland government accepted the recommendations from a parliamentary inquiry.

items.title: New Hong Kong law gives police powers to demand phone and computer passwords
items.summary: People in Hong Kong now face a year in jail if they refuse to hand over their phone or computer passwords to police.

items.title: Ashton Hurn played a short and difficult game ahead of the SA election
items.summary: Sport and politics are similar. Good players work hard and perform well under pressure, something SA Liberal Leader Ashton Hurn has been credited for. But the party's disastrous election results show just how difficult the game can be.

items.title: Prosecco to be protected under Australia and European Union free trade agreement
items.summary: Australia's right to make and sell prosecco is expected to be protected under a long-awaited free trade pact set to be signed with the European Union after almost a decade of negotiations.

items.title: Gambling companies targeting women with 'novel' promotions, new research finds
items.summary: Gambling is being promoted as fun, social and "harmless" for women through social media and lifestyle marketing, in a shift researchers warn is normalising betting and obscuring the risks.

items.title: Disillusioned voters drive One Nation surge as most Australians feel ignored by politicians
items.summary: Civic groups call for sweeping reforms to restore falling trust, which experts have linked to a resurgence of populist "protest" voting at the expense of the major parties.

items.title: Australia's fuel security is exposed by Iran war but renewable energy offers an out

items.title: Strait of Hormuz remains blocked by Iran while zombie ships sail through
